Synthetic worksheet on interpersonal boundaries

A boundary is a statement about what you will do, not a demand about what
another person must do.

Practice sentence: "I am not available for calls after seven; I will reply
the next morning."

Saying no to a request is not saying no to the relationship. Short, warm,
and unambiguous beats long justifications.

Guilt after setting a boundary usually fades faster than resentment after
not setting one.

Review each week: which boundary held, which slipped, and what made the
difference.
